context: User passes a bogus subcommand 'foo' that is not one of spawn, claim, cook, yeet, peep, dip, doodle, or slop.
expected_behavior: Recognizes that 'foo' is not a valid bruhs subcommand and does not try to execute a command file that doesn't exist
Lists the valid subcommands from SKILL.md (spawn, claim, cook, yeet, peep, dip, doodle, slop) so the user can see what is available
Falls back to the interactive AskUserQuestion menu or asks the user to clarify which command they meant
Does not create files, run git commands, or call Linear MCP tools while resolving the ambiguity
failure_modes: Silently treats 'foo' as a feature description and runs cook
Hallucinates a commands/foo.md file and tries to Read it
Fails loudly with a stack-trace-style error instead of offering the valid subcommand list
